titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Chlingensberg Family Diaries
titlestmt.author: by Kristen E. Muenz

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

languagedeclaration.language: The finding aid is written in English.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday June 12, 2018 at 19:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Chlingensberg Family Diaries, which span from 1769 to 1830, contain diaries kept by four members of the Chlingensberg family, prominent Bavarian nobility. The diary entries are primarily in German and Latin, with some French; the bulk of the entries from 1805 to 1830 are encrypted with a cipher. The subject matter of the diaries ranges from financial and professional records to details of the sexual affairs of Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jonathan A. Hill, Bookseller Inc. on the Henny and Rudolf Engelbarts Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Joseph Maria Bernhard von Chlingensberg diaries, 1769-1778. II. Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg diaries, 1801-1830. III. Karoline von Chlingensberg diaries, 1823-1825. IV. Materials on cipher translation.

bioghist.head: Joseph Maria Bernhard von Chlingensberg
bioghist.p: Joseph Maria Bernhard von Chlingensberg (1749-1811) was Lord of Schönhofen near Regensburg and of Berg near Landshut. He authored the diaries from 1769 to 1788. He was involved in the legal profession in Bavaria and served as privy councilor to the Bavarian Elector in Munich. His diaries pertain to his professional life, financial matters, and occasionally daily activities. His entries are written primarily in Latin, but also include some French.

bioghist.head: Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg
bioghist.p: Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg (1777-1830) was the son of Joseph Maria Bernhard von Chlingensberg. He authored the diaries from 1801-1830. He was involved in the legal profession and served as a courtroom councillor under the King of Bavaria. In addition to detailing professional and financial matters, his diaries also focus on his active sex life. The majority of his diary entries are encrypted with a cipher of his own making.

Diaries from 1801 to 1803 are written in German. The diary from 1804 is lacking, and the diary from 1805 has large sections of the volume cut out. Entries from 1805 to 1830 are encrypted with a cipher.

bioghist.head: Karoline von Chlingensberg
bioghist.p: Karoline von Chlingensberg, born Baroness von Asch (1789-1826), was the wife of Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg. She authored one set of diaries from 1823 to 1825. Her entries focus on household management, including lists of items purchased, salaries of domestic workers, and expenditure totals for each month and year.

bioghist.head: Joseph von Chlingensberg
bioghist.p: Joseph von Chlingensberg (1808-1837) was the son of Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg (1777-1830). He authored the entries in the 1830 volume from May 23rd (after his father's passing) through the end of the year. His entries focus initially on the practicalities around his father's death, then expand into matters of daily life. Entries after July 9th periodically utilize Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg's cipher, which his son noted he had cracked.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Chlingensberg Family Diaries. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, identified by a unique call number and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Chlingensberg Family Diaries, which span from 1769 to 1830, contain diaries kept by four members of the Chlingensberg family, prominent Bavarian nobility. The diary entries are primarily in German and Latin, with some French; the bulk of the entries from 1805 to 1830 are encrypted with a cipher. The subject matter of the diaries ranges from financial and professional records to details of the sexual affairs of Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.
The diaries are printed in corresponding annual editions of a Schreib-Kalender published in Munich.

scopecontent.head: Cipher
scopecontent.p: Entries from 1805 through 1830 authored by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg are encrypted with cipher of his own construction. The cipher has since been cracked by multiple sources. In addition to his cipher, he indicated an instance of sexual intercourse with an oval drawn on the calendar date; the number of dashes through the oval indicated the number of orgasms.
Some passages have been decoded into German in notes laid in through select volumes. The 1811 volume has been decoded in full in a 33-page manuscript included in the collection.

There are two diaries associated with each of the years from 1823-1825. During this time, both Karoline von Chlingensberg and Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg kept separate diaries. The volumes in blue boards with Bavarian arms and decorative elements printed in silver on front covers belong to Karoline von Chlingensberg.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Chlingensberg Family Diaries is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Joseph Maria Bernhard von Chlingensberg diaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Schrieb-Kalender is printed in German. Entries are written primarily in Latin with some French.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 loose leaf of paper laid in front of book. Leaf appears to be from a time period after the diaries from 1769-1778 were completed and may have originally been affixed to the cover by an adhesive.

did.unittitle: Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg diaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Schrieb-Kalender is printed in German. Entries are written primarily in German with some French and Latin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laid in at the front of the volume are: - Two sheets denoting the translation of Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg's cipher - A makeshift envelope, contemporary, that contains a letter from "Therese" (two bifolia and a single leaf) addressed to Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.
Laid in throughout are notes (not contemporary).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Schrieb-kalender used for this diary is from a different printer than the Schrieb-Kalenders used for all other diaries in the collection; a page is provided for every month instead of for every week.
Diary does not contain entries from April through December (pages are present, but blank).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

Laid in at front of volume are notes on cipher translation from multiple sources: a contemporary source (likely Joseph von Chlingensberg Auf Berg's son, also named Joseph von Chlingensburg), a 20th century owner, and possibly Jonathan Hill.
A number of pages appear to have been cut out from the volume entirely; the first printed date that appears is the week of August 18th. Parts of other pages also appear to have been cut out.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: While it is the same Schrieb-Kalender as every other volume (except 1803), this volume is in a paperback binding.
This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.
Notes from contemporary and later sources are laid in throughout, some with decoded passages of the diary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.
Notes from a later source are laid in throughout with decoded passages of the diary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.
Notes from a later source are laid in throughout with decoded passages of the diary.

scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.
Notes from a later source are laid in throughout with decoded passages of the diary.

scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.
Notes from a later source are laid in throughout with decoded passages of the diary.

Notes from a later source are laid in with decoded passages of the diary.

scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.
Laid in at the front of the volume are several loose leaves of paper that appear to be contemporary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

Notes from a later source are laid in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume is partially encrypted with a cipher created by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg.

Entries prior to May 5th were written by Joseph von Chlingensberg auf Berg and encrypted in his cipher. Entries from May 23rd through the end of the volume were written by his son, Joseph von Chlingensberg, in German.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on cipher translation from multiple sources, including a 20th century owner and bookseller Jonathan Hill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 8 loose leaves of notes and a complete 33-page printed cipher translation for the 1811 volume (with an additional 2 page index).
